description: Unable to rest or relax due to anxiety or excess energy. 'Wipha' names the quality of restlessness—the body or mind that cannot settle, energy seeking outlet.
sound_symbolism: The word cannot settle. 'Wi' begins with restless energy, stillness impossible. Then 'ha' /ɸa/ breathes with agitation, restlessness present.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'wipha thaemo' (restless heart). Opposite of 'moesha' (still). Names a state requiring attention—restlessness often signals unmet need.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Restlessness (uddhacca) is a hindrance—but noticing it is the first step toward settling.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Restless communication rushes—the agitated mind speaks before thinking, reacts before understanding.
